title: Tedryn Holocron
content:

Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]], Pages 7-11. |culture=[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] |creators=[[Tedryn]] {{C|Possible}}[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] |created=More than four thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] |destroyed=[[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]][[Dark Baas]] *[[Ashka Boda]][[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]], The Secret of Ashka Boda *[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] *[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]''[[Jedi Search]]'', Epilogue *[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] |keepers=*Tedryn *[[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] *[[Ood Bnar]][[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] *Vodo-Siosk Baas *Bodo Baas |locations=*[[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] *[[Byss/Legends|Byss]] *[[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] |size=Palm-sized |shape=Cube |color=[[Color/Legends|Milky jade]][[Tionne Solusar]]'s own description from [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] |material=[[Crystal/Legends|Crystal]] |length= |width= |height= |weight= |protection= |inscription= |markings= |contents= |heritage=Jedi |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[New Jedi Order]] }} {{Quote|…some teachings of the holocron can be dangerous before you are ready.|Vodo-Siosk Baas to Exar Kun|}} The '''Tedryn Holocron''', also known as '''Vodo-Siosk Baas's holocron''', the '''Holocron of''' '''Bodo Baas''', or simply '''the Jedi Holocron''', was a [[Jedi holocron/Legends|Jedi holocron]] created over four thousand years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], possibly by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tedryn]]. ==Properties== [[File:Tedryn_holocron_activating.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Leia activated the holocron by touching the design on its side.]] The [[Tedryn]] [[Holocron/Legends|Holocron]] was a glowing cube that sat comfortably in the palm of the [[Limb/Legends|hand]].[[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]] It was a milky [[Jade/Legends|jade]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] block with ancient [[Writing/Legends|writings]] on its smooth surface.''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', Page 62. The Jedi teaching device was an interactive wisdom source, that combined ancient organic crystalline formations and hardware. Its metal parts were embossed with curious designs suggesting great antiquity. The inner crystals sensed the affiliation of the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-user]] to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]], though any [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-talented user could activate the holocron by touching the design on its side. The holocron produced a primitive green-tinted [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of its primary [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper]]. The knowledge contained in the holocron could be accessed by anyone to a certain extent. On the most superficial level the holocron was merely a recording device, a collection of records with interactive holograms of the authors. The basic knowledge in the holocron could be accessed directly, like a computer [[encyclopedia]], or by interfacing with the current gatekeeper. However, the greatest portion of the wisdom could be unlocked only by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. The inner organic crystals were the key to the deeper levels of knowledge. Not even a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]—such as [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], who possessed the device for thirty years—had the power to reach its restricted information. Deeper teachings and accounts were relayed directly by the Masters who added them to the holocron. The Tedryn Holocron was tailored toward helping Jedi Masters penetrate the secrets of the Force. Even beings already [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the Jedi ways benefit greatly from such a source of learning.''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', Page 5. ==Gatekeepers== The gatekeeper was a Jedi Master's holographic simulation that worked as a search and recovery engine built in every holocron. The fact that one gatekeeper relayed certain information was not an indicator that this information had been recorded by that particular gatekeeper, as the data contained within the holocron could be accessed by all gatekeepers. Originally, the holocron was named after '''Tedryn''', but later often was referred to as simply the Jedi Holocron, or the Holocron of [[Bodo Baas]]. The [[New Jedi Order]] called it the Tedryn Holocron to avoid confusion over [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] and Bodo Baas, and also to honor their predecessor. Tedryn may have been the creator and first gatekeeper of the holocron. At some point before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], Jedi Master '''[[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]]''', one of the gatekeepers, added a record to the holocron about the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. [[File:TedrynHolocron_EGTF.jpg|thumb|right|130px|[[Bodo Baas]], one of the holocron's gatekeepers]] The Tedryn Holocron eventually passed to '''Vodo-Siosk Baas''', a [[Krevaaki]] Jedi Master and [[Jedi historian]]. In [[3997 BBY]], his hologram introduced the recent history of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], including [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Freedon Nadd]] to [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]. Vodo's hologram introduced Exar Kun, [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and the Great Sith War to the students of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] more than [[11 ABY/Legends|four thousand years]] after his death. Later Luke asked Baas about the fate of Exar Kun, and the gatekeeper told him that Kun slew his own master, Vodo himself, when the latter tried to bring his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] back from the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Vodo-Siosk Baas was the last invoked gatekeeper, because the spirit of Kun destroyed the holocron before Baas could reveal Exar's fate to Skywalker. An auxiliary gatekeeper told stories of intrigues in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to the apprentices of Skywalker''[[I, Jedi]]'' before another gatekeeper spoke to Luke of how the young [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] became a Jedi. The last gatekeeper of the Holocron was '''Bodo Baas''', a descendant of Vodo-Siosk Baas. His hologram explained a partial story of Ulic-Qel Droma, a brief description of the dark side, and the prophecy written by [[Bodo Baas's Master|Baas' master]].[[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]] Later Baas suggested that ancient Jedi artifacts may be found on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], an old Jedi teaching center, and that they could find survivors of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] Later, in the Jedi Praxeum, Luke asked Baas to explain the power classified as [[Mind trick/Legends|Alter Mind]] to [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] in the apprentice's chamber. ==Contents== According to Obi-Wan Kenobi, the Tedryn Holocron contained thousand-year-old secrets and the some of the most secret Jedi history and teachings. It held information about the origins of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and their earliest conflicts. Vodo-Siosk Baas made a recording about the war known as the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]], in which he spoke of the [[Second Great Schism]], how the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] used the dark side to turn animals into warriors, mounts, and [[Leviathan (creature)|Leviathans]], and the [[Battle of Corbos|final confrontation on Corbos]]. By the time of the recording, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had learned that the [[Exiles|exiled Dark Jedi]] had conquered the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] and that [[Ajunta Pall]] had formed an [[Sith Empire/Legends|Empire]]. Sometime after 5,000 BBY, Master Odan-Urr asked [[Jori Daragon]], [[Anavus Svag]] and the [[Dark Holocron]] about the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], before making a record to preserve the full story of the war for the Jedi Order. In the record he spoke of hyperspace explorers Gav and Jori Daragon of the Koros system meeting the descendants of the Exiles, who became Dark Lords of the Sith Empire. Odan-Urr spoke of Naga Sadow launching an assault using illusions to frighten off the Republic troops. Once the Sith were defeated, Odan-Urr recovered the Dark Holocron from a ship and got to know Sadow's escape. At some point before the Great Sith War, when he was around a thousand years old, Ood Bnar recorded his prophecies in the holocron.[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] The device also contained information about Ood Bnar himself, the Neti species and the Ryyk planet.[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']], Page 88.
'''This section includes possible contents from the [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: Essential Guide to the Force'']], where Tionne states that the quotes are taken either from the [[Great Holocron/Legends|Great Holocron]], [[Asli Krimsan's holocron]], or the Tedryn Holocron.'''
Arca Jeth made a record about the Freedon Nadd Uprising, in which he spoke of Freedon Nadd and his heirs, King Ommin and Queen Amanoa, and of Jeth's students: Tott Doneeta, Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma. Jeth noted, that Nadd gave his support to the founders of the Krath: Satal and Aleema Keto. {{Endgame}} According to Bodo Baas, three Jedi Knights''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', Page 66. tried to conquer the dark side by learning its secrets, but all three of them perished. Vodo-Siosk Baas made a record about the Great Sith War, caused by Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma. [[Bodo Baas]] retold the saga of [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] to Leia Organa Solo, but for an unknown reason, with some differences. *3958-3956 BBY: Bodo Baas about the Second Sith War: Mandalorian Wars, Rakata, Korriban, Revan, Malak, Star Forge, Bastila Shan, Sion, Kreia, Nihilus, Brianna, Atris, Visas Marr, Ruin *2000 BBY: Bodo Baas about Darth Ruin *c. 1010 BBY: Jedi Master Pernicar about Wud Mortull, the New Sith Wars and the Sith lightsaber *580 BBY: Bodo Baas about the Zeison Sha *580 BBY: Bodo Baas about the Matukai
'''This section includes possible contents from the [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: Essential Guide to the Force'']], where Tionne states that the quotes are taken either from the [[Great Holocron/Legends|Great Holocron]], [[Asli Krimsan's holocron]], or the Tedryn Holocron.'''
before 796 BBY Rekpa De's many statement on lightsaber duels *[[3996 BBY]], Tolaris Shim about Ulic and the four stages of the dark side *[[3951 BBY]], Deesra Luur Jada about the reformation of Revan and about Dantooine {{Endgame}} *Bodo Baas about the dark side *Luke found some clues in the Holocron, which made him believe that some Jedi survived [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Vader's program of extermination]]. *The Holocron revealed to Luke that the planet Ossus in the [[Adega system/Legends|Adega system]] was a center of Jedi learning for thousands of years. *Stories of the young Yoda becoming a Jedi. *Bodo Baas spoke about the three areas of the Force and the power of Alter Mind. *Stories of Old Republic intrigues. *Prophecy of [[Bodo Baas's Master]] about the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker twins]] and the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. *Bodo Baas about training a Jedi *Odan-Urr about the Force Sever power
'''This section includes possible contents from the [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: Essential Guide to the Force'']], where Tionne states that the quotes are taken either from the [[Great Holocron/Legends|Great Holocron]], [[Asli Krimsan's holocron]], or the Tedryn Holocron.'''
*Odan-Urr about patience *Odan-Urr about how a Jedi must behave in public *Rekpa De's many statement on lightsaber duels *[[3996 BBY]], Tolaris Shim about Ulic and the four stages of the dark side {{Endgame}} ==History== ===Early history=== The Holocron was created sometimes before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], possibly by Jedi Master [[Tedryn]], whom the artifact was named for. Tedryn might have been the first gatekeeper of the holocron and was followed by a long line of Jedi who contributed to the Holocron. One of the early gatekeepers was Master Odan-Urr, who added a record to the holocron about the Great Hyperspace War. One other gatekeeper was the then thousand years old Ood Bnar, who recorded his prophecies in the Holocron. By [[3997 BBY]], [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] was in possession of the Holocron and became a gatekeeper by imparting it with his knowledge and memory. One day, he found his Apprentice, Exar Kun questioning the holocron about the Dark Jedi Freedon Nadd and the Sith. Baas turned off the device, feeling that such knowledge was meant only for Jedi Masters. Around [[600 BBY/Legends|600 BBY]], Bodo Baas inherited the Holocron and reorganized every single recording.{{FactFile|74|BAA 1-2|Bodo Baas}} {{C|German version}} By [[590 BBY]], he was part of a group of Jedi who lived in the Adega system and recorded the teachings and history of the Jedi. The Holocron was believed to have gone missing hundreds of years before the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], but it was in the possession of Ashka Boda. ===Imperial Period=== In 19 BBY, at the beginning of the Great Jedi Purge, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] agents brought Jedi Masters before him to be questioned and tortured for forgotten secrets and cures, that could help him in his quest for immortality. The Tedryn Holocron was among the belongings of Bodo Baas, who was executed by the Emperor.''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'', Page 9. Shortly thereafter the device came in the possession of Master Ashka Boda. The Dark Lord executed Boda as well, and took possession of the Tedryn Holocron. Palpatine used the secrets in the holocron to cheat death with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] technology, and he recorded some of the holocron's secrets in his [[The Book of Anger|''Book of Anger'']].[[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]], The Book of Anger However, the greater portion of the ancient knowledge remained hidden from the Dark Lord, because it was accessible only by Jedi Knights. He considered the Holocron to be only a collection of ancient record. The reborn Emperor held the artifact in his study in the [[Emperor's Citadel|Imperial Citadel]] on Byss.''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', Page 36. At some later time, Obi-Wan Kenobi described the Holocron to his apprentice, Luke Skywalker. Kenobi thought the holocron could help reestablish the Jedi Order. ===Rediscovery for the New Jedi Order=== [[File:Leia_Bodo.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] listens to the teachings of the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]].]] In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] was captured by the reborn Emperor on his throneworld, Byss.[[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]] The Sith Lord showed the Tedryn Holocron to the Jedi, trying unsuccessfully to turn her to the dark side. Leia managed to flee with the holocron. Onboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] she listened to Bodo Baas's version of the downfall of Ulic Qel-Droma, and wondered whether there was hope for Luke, or he had gone too far in the dark side in his effort to conquer it from within. After the [[Battle of Calamari|battle of Mon Calamari]], Leia returned to [[Pinnacle Base]], where she again accessed the holocron. She asked Master Baas about her brother's future, but the gatekeeper answered that he could not tell because Skywalker's future was obscured. He then recounted a prophecy written by his own Master. Moments later, a fleet of Star Destroyers led by the ''Eclipse'' dropped out of hyperspace above the Pinnacle Moon. The reborn Emperor demanded that Leia and the Tedryn Holocron be delivered to him; he would discuss a truce if the Rebels complied. Leia accepted his terms, but left the holocron in her chambers. Aboard the ''Eclipse'', she realized that Sidious's hold over Luke had become too strong, and that the dark side was consuming him. The siblings dueled until Leia managed to break Sidious's spell over Luke, and they confronted the Dark Lord together, fulfilling Bodo Baas's ancient prophecy. Later, Luke found clues in the Tedryn Holocron indicating possible survivors of the Jedi Purge. He spent many standard weeks searching for Jedi, until eventually, he found [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Kam Solusar]] on the [[Nespis VIII|Space City]] of [[Nespis VIII]]. Meanwhile, Leia spent long hours with the Tedryn Holocron while Luke was away. During one such session, Baas told her that [[Vima-Da-Boda]] could help them. Luke learned from the Holocron that ancient Jedi artifacts were possibly located on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. His new ally Kam Solusar was rather unimpressed with the small cube, but he was taken aback when it sensed him and activated. The image of Baas gave some cryptic wisdom, and instructed Kam and his fellow Jedi to have patience. Skywalker and Solusar traveled to Ossus, where they med Ood Bnar, an hibernating Jedi Master from Exar Kun's time, whose prophecies are stored in the Holocron. With the help of the native [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]], the Jedi found a Jedi library that had remained undisturbed for four thousand years.[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] By 11 ABY, the Tedryn Holocron was kept in a small library, where Luke Skywalker's apprentices could study it. Skywalker gathered his twelve students in the grand audience chamber of the Massassi temple on Yavin 4 for a formal opening of the Jedi Praxeum. He invoked gatekeeper Vodo-Siosk Baas to impart a teaching to his apprentices. The ancient Krevaaki Jedi Master spoke of the Great Sith War, and of Exar Kun and Ulic-Qel Droma, and he warned the students to beware of the temptations of conquest. Tionne Solusar studied Jedi history from the holocron in the month after arriving to the Praxeum, transcribing its data to tapes and holograms, with a sense of urgency. Her studies allowed her to help the other students with historical examples when the instructions of Master Skywalker were not clear enough. After Gantoris constructed a lightsaber, Corran Horn suspected that he had learned how from the Tedryn Holocron, but Luke explained to him that an inbuilt restriction mechanism prevented the user from accessing knowledge for which he or she is not ready. After the death of Gantoris, Horn advised Master Skywalker on repeating the historical lessons of the Holocron to the students so as to instill a sense of history and purpose to the students, and make them less vulnerable for the corruption of the dark side. Bodo Baas spoke to the Jedi hopefuls about the heroic adventures of ancient Jedi Knights and about their battles against the dark side. Corran Horn tried to get some information about the murder of Gantoris, and Baas explained to him that, at the height of the Sith Wars, Yavin 4 had been the seat of Sith Lord Exar Kun. Kun enslaved the Massassi and forced them to build temples in the jungle, as Naga Sadow had done before. Eventually, the Dark Lord was killed in a Jedi attack. An auxiliary gatekeeper told the students stories of court intrigue in the Old Republic without specifying the subjects of his tales. After Corran Horn and Tionne had accessed all the information concerning Exar Kun, Luke tried to access the deeper levels of the device, but when he asked the Vodo-Siosk Baas gatekeeper about Exar Kun's fate, the spirit of the Dark Lord, who had been imprisoned on Yavin 4 since his death four millennia earlier, interrupted Vodo-Siosk Baas by destroying the Holocron. Transcripts and copied data collected by Tionne Solusar and stored in the [[Jedi Archives (Ossus)|new Jedi library]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], kept the teachings of the Tedryn Holocron alive after its destruction. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|The Tedryn Holocron is the one that Vodo is in possession of in ''Tales of the Jedi'', and he's ''one'' of the gatekeepers of it. ''One'' of the gatekeepers of the Jedi Holocron is Bodo Baas. Ood Bnar is ''another'' gatekeeper of that same Jedi Holocron.|[[Bob Cooper]] paraphrasing [[Kevin J. Anderson]]|[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]] {{C|"Rebel Writers" letters column}}}} The Tedryn Holocron was first mentioned in the [[Dark Empire endnotes|endnotes]] for [[Dark Empire 2]], which was written by [[Tom Veitch]] and was published on [[February 18]], [[1992]]. There it's referred to as an "ancient object" that Palpatine learned Essence Transfer from and no further information is given about what it is. It went on to be mentioned again in the endnotes for [[Dark Empire 4]], which was published on [[June 23]],[[1992]] and to appear in [[Dark Empire 5]], which was published on [[August 18]], [[1992]] and in [[Dark Empire 6]], which was published on [[October 20]], [[1992]]. Throughout [[Star Wars: Dark Empire|Dark Empire]] and the [[Dark Empire endnotes]], the Tedryn Holocron is referred to as "the Jedi Holocron". It's also referred to as such in [[Star Wars: Dark Empire II|Dark Empire II]], the [[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy|Jedi Academy Trilogy]], and [[Firestorm (short story)|Firestorm]]. The opening crawls for [[Tales of the Jedi 1]], [[Tales of the Jedi 3]], and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1]] refer to "the Jedi Holocron", which was implied to be the Tedryn Holocron by no other holocrons being known of at the time of their publication. However, [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith]] refers to the Tedryn Holocron as "a holocron" and also refers to a [[Dark Holocron|Sith holocron]], which went on to appear in [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith 4]]. The name "Tedryn Holocron" is used for the first time in [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War 1]] and went on to be used in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]. In [[The Essential Guide to Characters]], the holocron that appears in [[Star Wars: Dark Empire|Dark Empire]] is said to be a different holocron than the one that appears in [[Dark Apprentice (novel)|Dark Apprentice]], but this contradicts the text of Dark Apprentice and therefore is assumed to be an error. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi 1|''Tales of the Jedi'' 1]] {{C|Mentioned in
